preamble: The transformation from a voiced retroflex fricative to an alveolar flap is a documented process of weakening and rhotacization, where a sibilant loses its frication and often its retroflexion to merge with alveolar rhotic liquids.

examples.note: Speakers exhibit free variation between a voiced retroflex fricative and an alveolar trill or flap.
The process of rhotacism involved the voicing of intervocalic sibilants to [z] before they weakened into rhotics (trills or flaps).

sources: Duanmu, S. (2000). The Phonology of Standard Chinese.
Weiss, M. (2009). Outline of the Historical and Comparative Grammar of Latin.
